title: Israel turns its back on France as Paris struggles to maintain dialogue
subhed: The Israeli Ministry of Defense announced on Tuesday that it would end its defense imports from France, implying that Paris was no longer counted among the country's allies. This move comes despite French hopes of repairing diplomatic ties damaged by France's recognition of the State of Palestine and amid the ongoing war in Lebanon.

French archives of Spaniards who fled Franco's dictatorship are now available online
subhed: From March 30, over 180,000 records are accessible on the French Office for the Protection of Refugees and Stateless Persons' website, due to funding from Spain aimed at preserving the memory of people's 40 years of exile in France.

title: France's far right splits over the question of sovereignty as several mayors take down EU flags
subhed: Several newly elected mayors from far-right leader Marine Le Pen's party have removed the European Union flag, blue with gold stars, from their town halls. The decision has sparked divisions between the nationalists.

title: Robert Capa, a photographer in the heat of the action, at Paris's Liberation Museum
subhed: A compelling exhibition, which features a montage of archival footage showing the work of Robert Capa, the Hungarian cofounder of Magnum Photos, details his extraordinary life.

title: Europeans remain powerless to tackle Orban, seen as too close to the Kremlin
subhed: With Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban, who has close ties with the Kremlin, vetoing new aid to Ukraine, European leaders hope their relations with Budapest will return to normal after Hungary's April 12 parliamentary elections.

War is transforming Ukraine's dogs who live on the front line
subhed: Canines found near combat zones are small, light and dark-colored, according to a study. These characteristics have helped them survive in a hostile environment.

title: Israel introduces death penalty for Palestinians accused of 'terrorist' murders
subhed: The law, passed by 62 lawmakers to 48, marks turning point in Israel's penal policy and further weakens the rule of law in the country.

title: Céline Dion picks Paris for her return to the stage after a six-year hiatus
subhed: After several days of hints, the Canadian star waited for her birthday to announce her first concert dates since 2020: She will be in residence for 10 nights at Paris La Défense Arena, between September 12 and October 14.
